design.prompt_shape: short leader skill plus triggered advanced references and compact views
design.machine_contracts: shared canonical schemas, validators, scripts, lifecycle state, and specify-runtime cognition runtime
design.passive_skills: classic passive prompt bundles are not installed; essential gates live in the owning SPX skill or advanced reference
design.classic_sources: audit inputs for SPX prompts; only the three classic map companion skills are also installed through the unchanged classic renderer

optimization_policy: preserve every classic command entrypoint, write boundary, state owner, explicit side-effect gate, and resumable stop point

preserve classic stage inputs, triggered outputs, state ownership, resume transitions, blocker semantics, failure recovery, and final-claim gates while leaving implementation strategy to the advanced agent
use no hard word or token ceiling; reduce duplication and tutorials only after required workflow semantics are represented in the owning skill, a triggered reference, or deterministic runtime
remove repeated cognition tutorials, fixed role chains, mandatory delegation, long examples, and model-authored stable boilerplate
delegate bounded bulk scanning to the lowest-cost capable worker while advanced leaders own scope, acceptance, conflicts, and escalation
use deterministic templates and runtimes for stable artifacts, validation, graph construction, and lifecycle transitions
install the unchanged classic sp-map-scan, sp-map-build, and sp-map-update skills beside SPX so low-cost models can run the established map workflow

spx-accept.optimization: context-restoring guided human end-to-end acceptance over a frozen requirement universe and Review-approved runtime targets, with every non-access failure routed first to a fresh Leader-owned Review/Fix/revalidation cycle

spx-analyze.optimization: read-only obligation analysis with stable blockers and one repair route

spx-ask.optimization: one bounded live-evidence pass without workflow state

spx-auto.optimization: one evidence-based continuation decision without a parallel state machine

spx-checklist.optimization: focused unit tests for written requirements, never implementation tests

spx-clarify.optimization: small decision batches repair an existing spec without replaying discovery

spx-constitution.optimization: constitution-only writes with targeted downstream impact reporting

spx-debug.optimization: ranked evidence-separating hypotheses replace a fixed multi-role chain

spx-deep-research.optimization: decision-scoped primary evidence and disposable spikes with a durable planning handoff

spx-design.optimization: one project-specific approved root design-system contract without generic starter anchoring

spx-discussion.optimization: semantic checkpoints and an exact confirmed handoff replace transcript ceremony

spx-explain.optimization: read the smallest authoritative artifact chain and explain decisions, not sections

spx-fast.optimization: leader-direct local edit with an explicit escalation gate

spx-implement.optimization: adaptive direct or native-subagent execution with evidence-backed Review handoff

spx-implement-teams.optimization: one implementation contract with explicit Codex and Claude durable-team backends

spx-integrate.optimization: readiness, overlap, sequencing, and combined verification before lane closeout

spx-map-build.optimization: validated scan input feeds deterministic graph construction and publication

spx-map-rebuild.optimization: optional orchestration of the independent scan and build skills

spx-map-scan.optimization: strict runtime packets send complete bulk reads to the lowest-cost capable workers

spx-map-update.optimization: leader-direct exact-path incremental maintenance with result-state gates

spx-plan.optimization: repository-grounded design decisions without research, task, or issue side effects

spx-prd.optimization: thin compatibility route to the canonical reconstruction scan and build skills

spx-prd-build.optimization: template-driven synthesis from a frozen validated scan package without rereading the repository

spx-prd-scan.optimization: low-cost evidence lanes with leader-owned L4 coverage and acceptance

spx-quick.optimization: compact resumable state with delegation only when it adds value

spx-review.optimization: real-entrypoint system validation with bounded repair, adaptive independent review lanes, and a fresh fingerprint gate

spx-research.optimization: thin compatibility route to spx-deep-research without duplicate state

spx-specify.optimization: planning-ready feature truth with a compact UI contract for every substantive UI change

spx-tasks.optimization: deterministic task-graph validation, structured UI task contracts, and just-in-time worker packet generation

spx-taskstoissues.optimization: explicit idempotent external export with a partial-retry boundary

spx-team.optimization: thin supported Codex runtime entrypoint separated from feature execution
